title: Specify discovery candidate
description: Validates a single `Candidate` block under `## Candidate inventory` in `discovery.md` per workflow Â§Discovery handshake. Each block has a kebab-case `id`, a non-empty `sources[]` list of source keys, a one-line `summary`, an optional `tentative` boolean flag set by `/spec:plan`'s `propose` sub-step when the agent merged the candidate across sources with low confidence, and (workflow Â§D6) an optional `aliases[]` list of kebab-case aliases. `slices[].sources[].candidate` resolves first against `id`, then against any `aliases[]` entry; aliases are stable across re-enumeration. An alias MUST NOT collide with another candidate's `id` or `aliases[]` in the same `discovery.md` (`discovery-alias-collision` from `specify slice validate`).

id.description: Stable kebab-case candidate identifier. Re-enumeration replaces by id.

sources.description: Non-empty list of source keys that surfaced this candidate (each entry matches a top-level `plan.yaml.sources.<key>` binding).

tentative.description: Optional. Set to `true` by `/spec:plan`'s `propose` sub-step when the cross-source merge that produced this candidate is uncertain; the operator reconciles at Gate 1.

aliases.description: workflow Â§D6 â€” optional kebab-case aliases for cross-source merge. `slices[].sources[].candidate` resolves a binding's candidate against the candidate's `id` or any `alias`. Authored by the source adapter at `enumerate` time or by `/spec:plan` at `propose` time when merging differently-named candidates across sources. Stable across re-enumeration; operator additions through `specify plan amend --add-alias` survive re-enumeration. Empty `aliases: []` and a missing field are equivalent.
